content.problemStatement:
You are given two numbers NNN and MMM. A pair of arrays (A,B)(A, B)(A,B) is called beautiful if and only if:


∣A∣=∣B∣=N|A| = |B| = N∣A∣=∣B∣=N (i.e. both their lengths are equal to NNN);
For all 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N, 1≤Ai≤M1 \le A_i \le M1≤Ai​≤M and 1≤Bi≤M1 \le B_i \le M1≤Bi​≤M;
median(A)≤median(B)median(A) \leq median(B)median(A)≤median(B);
AAA is lexicographically smaller than BBB.
Find the number of **beautiful** pairs of arrays. Since this number can be huge, print it modulo MODMODMOD.

Note:


median(X)median(X)median(X) denotes the ⌈∣X∣2⌉th\lceil \frac{|X|}{2} \rceil ^{th}⌈2∣X∣​⌉th element after sorting the array XXX.
An array XXX is lexicographically smaller than YYY if and only if there exists some index iii such that Xi<YiX_i \lt Y_iXi​<Yi​ and Xj=YjX_j = Y_jXj​=Yj​ for 1≤j<i1 \le j \lt i1≤j<i.

Input Format
The first and only line of input contains three integers NNN, MMM, MODMODMOD - the parameters defined in the statement.



Output Format
Output a single integer - the number of beautiful pairs modulo MODMODMOD.
